modes.title: Two modes. One truth.
modes.subtitle: Whether you're clearing a release or building a case, PROBATIO adapts to your workflow.

screening.label: Screening Mode
screening.title: Release with confidence
screening.description: Upload your track and get a comprehensive similarity report in minutes. PROBATIO scans against a vast reference database, flagging potential conflicts across melody, harmony, rhythm, and structure — before your music goes public.

features.risk: Clear risk classification from low to critical

forensic.label: Forensic Mode
forensic.title: Build an airtight case
forensic.description: When disputes escalate, PROBATIO delivers court-ready evidence packages with statistical significance testing, expert musicologist review, and complete chain-of-custody documentation that has been admitted in federal court.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: From upload to insight in four steps.

upload.title: Upload
upload.description: Drop your audio file — WAV, MP3, FLAC, or any major format. We normalize everything to ensure consistent analysis.

separate.title: Separate & Extract
separate.description: Our AI isolates vocals, drums, bass, and melodic elements using state-of-the-art stem separation, then extracts spectral features from each layer.

analyze.title: Analyze & Match
analyze.description: Advanced algorithms compare your track across four dimensions — melody, harmony, rhythm, and structure — against millions of reference works.

report.title: Report & Act
report.description: Receive a detailed risk assessment with similarity scores, matched segments, and clear recommendations. Export or escalate to forensic review.

evidence.title: The evidence speaks for itself
evidence.subtitle: PROBATIO's analysis has been tested in real-world legal proceedings.
evidence.quote: PROBATIO's forensic report was the single most compelling piece of evidence in our case. The jury understood the data immediately.
evidence.quoteAuthor: — Partner, Entertainment Litigation, Top-10 AmLaw Firm

forWho.title: Built for the people who make and protect music
forWho.subtitle: From bedroom producers to boardroom litigators.

artists.title: Artists & Producers
artists.description: Scan your beats before you release. Know your risk before the label asks. Protect yourself from accidental infringement — and from those who'd claim your originality as theirs.

labels.title: Labels & Publishers
labels.description: Clear every release in your catalog. Integrate PROBATIO into your A&R workflow via API. Reduce legal exposure and accelerate your release timeline with confidence.

legal.title: Attorneys & Expert Witnesses
legal.description: Build stronger cases with court-ready forensic evidence. Full chain of custody. Statistical significance testing. Expert musicologist review. Evidence packages admitted in federal court.

distributors.title: Distributors & DSPs
distributors.description: Screen catalog-scale submissions with our API. Flag potential disputes before they become takedowns. Protect your platform and your artists.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.subtitle: Start free. Scale with your needs. No hidden fees.

free.name: Free
free.description: Try PROBATIO on your next release.

starter.name: Starter
starter.description: For independent artists and producers shipping regularly.

professional.name: Professional
professional.description: For labels, publishers, and legal teams.

standard.name: Forensic Standard
standard.description: Automated forensic comparison with court-ready documentation.

expert.name: Forensic Expert
expert.description: Full expert review with witness availability.

whatIs.question: What exactly does PROBATIO analyze?
whatIs.answer: PROBATIO performs a multi-dimensional analysis of your audio across four key dimensions: melody (pitch contour and intervals), harmony (chord progressions and tonal content), rhythm (timing patterns and groove), and structure (song form and arrangement). Each dimension is scored independently and combined into an overall similarity assessment.

howAccurate.question: How accurate is the analysis?
howAccurate.answer: PROBATIO achieves a 99.4% classification accuracy rate across our test corpus of over 50,000 known pairs. Our forensic reports include statistical significance testing with p-values, ensuring results meet the evidentiary standards required by courts.

courtAdmissible.question: Has PROBATIO evidence been used in court?
courtAdmissible.answer: Yes. PROBATIO forensic evidence packages have been admitted in 47 copyright proceedings in the United States, including federal court. Our chain-of-custody documentation and expert review process are designed to meet Daubert standard requirements.

fileFormats.question: What audio formats do you support?
fileFormats.answer: We support WAV, MP3, FLAC, AAC, OGG, M4A, AIFF, and WMA. For best results, we recommend uploading lossless formats (WAV or FLAC). All uploads are normalized to a consistent format for analysis.

howLong.question: How long does an analysis take?
howLong.answer: Standard screening takes 2-5 minutes. Deep analysis takes 5-15 minutes. Forensic analysis with expert review takes 3-5 business days. Processing times depend on file length, complexity, and current queue depth.

privacy.question: Is my music kept private?
privacy.answer: Absolutely. Your uploads are encrypted at rest and in transit. Audio files are processed in isolated environments and are never added to our reference database without your explicit consent. We are SOC 2 Type II compliant.

api.question: Do you offer an API?
api.answer: Yes. Our REST API allows you to integrate PROBATIO analysis into your existing workflow — A&R tools, DAWs, distribution platforms, or legal case management systems. API access is available on Starter plans and above.

refund.question: What's your refund policy?
refund.answer: Subscription plans can be canceled at any time, effective at the end of the current billing period. Forensic analysis fees are non-refundable once an expert has been assigned to your case. Unused credits roll over for up to 90 days.

cta.title: Your next release deserves certainty.

low.label: Low Risk
low.description: No significant similarities detected. Your track appears to be original.
low.recommendation: You're clear to release. Low similarity is within normal creative variation.

moderate.label: Moderate Risk
moderate.description: Some similarities detected that may warrant attention.
moderate.recommendation: Review the matched segments carefully. Consider consulting with a music attorney if the similarities are in prominent sections.

high.label: High Risk
high.description: Significant similarities detected across multiple dimensions.
high.recommendation: We strongly recommend legal review before release. Consider modifying the flagged sections or obtaining a license from the rights holder.

critical.label: Critical Risk
critical.description: Substantial similarity detected. High likelihood of infringement claim.
critical.recommendation: Do not release without legal counsel. The similarities are substantial enough to support a copyright infringement claim. Consider a forensic analysis for detailed evidence.

legal.disclaimer: PROBATIO provides similarity analysis for informational purposes. Analysis results do not constitute legal advice. Similarity scores indicate statistical likelihood of overlap, not legal determinations of infringement. Always consult with a qualified attorney for legal matters.
legal.copyright: © {year} Clandestino Ventures, LLC. All rights reserved.
legal.evidenceDisclaimer: Evidence packages are generated using automated analysis tools. While designed to meet court evidentiary standards, admissibility depends on jurisdiction and judicial discretion. Expert review packages include human expert analysis.
legal.privacyNotice: Your audio files are encrypted at rest and in transit. Files are processed in isolated environments and are never shared or added to reference databases without explicit consent.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.subtitle: The legal agreement governing your use of PROBATIO, a forensic music copyright intelligence platform operated by Clandestino Ventures, LLC.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.p1: By accessing or using PROBATIO — including our website, application, API, or any related services — you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you are using PROBATIO on behalf of an organization, you represent that you have the authority to bind that organization to these terms. If you do not agree to these terms, you must not use the platform.

serviceDescription.title: 2. Service Description
serviceDescription.p1: PROBATIO is a forensic music copyright intelligence platform that analyzes audio content for similarity across melodic, harmonic, rhythmic, and structural dimensions. The platform uses machine learning models, signal processing algorithms, and vector similarity search to produce quantitative similarity scores and risk assessments.
serviceDescription.p2: PROBATIO operates in two primary modes: Screening Mode for pre-release similarity checks, and Forensic Mode for detailed, court-oriented evidence packages with chain of custody documentation.
serviceDescription.warning: PROBATIO is a technical screening and analysis tool. It is not a legal service, does not practice law, and does not provide legal opinions. Similarity scores are mathematical computations, not legal conclusions.

userResponsibilities.title: 3. User Responsibilities & Audio Upload Rights
userResponsibilities.intro: When using PROBATIO, you represent and warrant that:

items.rights: You have the legal right to upload the audio content you submit — whether as the copyright owner, an authorized licensee, an agent acting on behalf of a rights holder, or under applicable fair use / fair dealing provisions.
items.mlProcessing: You understand that uploaded audio will be processed through machine learning pipelines, including stem separation, feature extraction, and embedding generation, for the purpose of similarity analysis.
items.noHarassment: You will not use PROBATIO's analysis results to harass, defame, or intimidate other creators, nor to pursue frivolous or bad-faith litigation designed to suppress legitimate artistic expression.
items.accuracy: The information you provide in your account and in connection with forensic cases is accurate and complete to the best of your knowledge.
items.compliance: Your use of PROBATIO complies with all applicable laws and regulations in your jurisdiction, including copyright law, data protection law, and rules governing electronic evidence.

noLegalAdvice.title: 4. No Legal Advice Disclaimer
noLegalAdvice.critical: THIS IS A CRITICAL PROVISION. PLEASE READ CAREFULLY.

items.notLegalAdvice: PROBATIO does NOT provide legal advice. No output of the platform — including similarity scores, risk classifications, forensic reports, or chain of custody documentation — constitutes legal counsel.
items.technicalOnly: Similarity scores are technical analyses representing mathematical comparisons between audio features. A high similarity score does not mean infringement has occurred. A low similarity score does not mean your work is free from all risk.
items.courtsDetermine: Only courts of competent jurisdiction can determine whether copyright infringement has occurred. The legal test for infringement involves factors beyond musical similarity, including access, independent creation, and the scope of copyright protection for the specific elements at issue.
items.consultCounsel: You should consult a qualified attorney before making any legal decisions based on PROBATIO's analysis. This is especially important before sending cease-and-desist letters, filing lawsuits, or making public allegations of copying.
items.noGuarantee: PROBATIO makes no guarantee regarding the legal outcome of any dispute, regardless of the similarity scores or risk levels reported by the platform.

noLegalAdvice.closing: By using PROBATIO, you acknowledge that you understand and accept this limitation. Clandestino Ventures, LLC, its officers, employees, and contractors are not liable for any legal action taken based on PROBATIO's analysis.

dataRetention.title: 5. Data Retention Policy
dataRetention.intro: We retain your data according to the following schedule, based on the type of analysis performed:
dataRetention.deletion: For Screening tier analyses, users may initiate deletion of their audio files at any time through account settings. Forensic data is subject to legal hold provisions and cannot be deleted while associated with an active or pending case.

intellectualProperty.title: 6. Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.p1: Intellectual property ownership under these terms is allocated as follows:

items.userRetains: You retain all intellectual property rights in audio content you upload. PROBATIO does not claim any ownership, license, or rights over your music beyond what is necessary to provide the analysis service.
items.noOwnership: PROBATIO retains no ownership interest in your audio. Uploaded files are processed, analyzed, and stored only for the purposes described in these terms and our Privacy Policy.
items.probatioIP: PROBATIO's analysis methodology, algorithms, machine learning models, pipeline architecture, user interface, and report templates are the intellectual property of Clandestino Ventures, LLC.
items.reportLicense: You receive a perpetual, non-exclusive license to use analysis reports generated from your submissions for any lawful purpose, including use in legal proceedings. You may not resell or redistribute PROBATIO reports as a standalone product.

forensicEvidence.title: 7. Forensic Evidence Disclaimer
forensicEvidence.p1: PROBATIO's Forensic Mode includes chain of custody documentation, cryptographic hash verification, and append-only audit logs designed to support the evidentiary integrity of analysis results. These features are built to meet the standards established in Daubert v. Merrell Dow Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
forensicEvidence.p2: However, these features support — they do not replace — proper legal procedures for evidence handling. The admissibility of PROBATIO's analysis in any legal proceeding is determined by the presiding judge or tribunal, based on the applicable rules of evidence and the specific circumstances of the case.
forensicEvidence.p3: PROBATIO makes no guarantee that any analysis, report, or evidence package will be admitted in any particular court or proceeding. Users and their legal counsel are responsible for ensuring that evidence is properly introduced in accordance with applicable procedural rules.

payment.title: 8. Payment & Refunds
payment.intro: Payment terms for PROBATIO services are as follows:

items.billing: Subscription plans are billed monthly via Stripe. Your subscription renews automatically unless canceled before the end of the current billing period.
items.forensicFees: Forensic analysis fees are one-time charges that must be paid in full before processing begins. Forensic pricing is published on our website and confirmed before you authorize the charge.
items.failureRefund: If the PROBATIO analysis pipeline fails to produce a result due to a system error, you will receive a full credit refund to your account. This credit can be applied to future analyses.
items.noRefundCompleted: Completed analyses are non-refundable. Once PROBATIO has successfully processed your audio and delivered a report, the analysis credit or fee is considered earned.
items.processor: All payments are processed by Clandestino Ventures, LLC through Stripe. We do not store your credit card information on our servers.

liability.title: 9. Limitation of Liability
liability.p1: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, PROBATIO AND CLANDESTINO VENTURES LLC ARE PROVIDED "AS IS" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.
liability.p2: IN NO EVENT SHALL CLANDESTINO VENTURES LLC, ITS OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, EMPLOYEES, CONTRACTORS, OR AFFILIATES BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARISING FROM YOUR USE OF PROBATIO, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO DAMAGES FOR LOST PROFITS, LOST DATA, BUSINESS INTERRUPTION, OR LEGAL COSTS INCURRED IN RELIANCE ON PROBATIO'S ANALYSIS.
liability.cap: Clandestino Ventures, LLC's total cumulative liability to you for all claims arising from or related to these terms or your use of PROBATIO shall not exceed the total fees you paid to PROBATIO in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.

governingLaw.title: 10. Governing Law
governingLaw.p1: These terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico and applicable federal laws of the United States, without regard to conflict of law principles.
governingLaw.p2: Any dispute arising from these terms or your use of PROBATIO shall be resolved in the federal or state courts located in Puerto Rico. You consent to the personal jurisdiction of such courts and waive any objection based on inconvenient forum.

contact.title: 11. Contact

terms.closing: These Terms of Service constitute the entire agreement between you and Clandestino Ventures, LLC regarding your use of PROBATIO. If any provision is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions continue in full force and effect. Our failure to enforce any right or provision does not constitute a waiver of that right or provision.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How PROBATIO collects, processes, stores, and protects your data. We believe transparency about data handling is non-negotiable, especially for a platform that processes copyrighted audio.

informationCollected.title: 1. Information We Collect
informationCollected.intro: PROBATIO collects the following categories of information to provide and improve our forensic music copyright intelligence service:

account.title: Account Information
account.email: Email address (used for authentication, billing, and service communications)
account.name: Full name (used for account identification and forensic report attribution)
account.organization: Organization name (optional; used for team features and invoice generation)
account.language: Language preference (English or Spanish; used to deliver localized interface and reports)

audio.title: Audio Files
audio.description: Audio files you upload for analysis are stored in encrypted cloud storage. Files are processed through our analysis pipeline and retained according to the schedule described in our Terms of Service. Audio files are never added to our reference database without your explicit, separate consent.

analysis.title: Analysis Data
analysis.results: Similarity analysis results including per-dimension scores (melody, harmony, rhythm, structure)
analysis.scores: Overall risk classification scores and confidence intervals
analysis.reports: Generated reports (PDF, CSV) including matched segments and recommendations
analysis.chainOfCustody: Chain of custody metadata: cryptographic hashes, timestamps, pipeline versions, and audit log entries

payment.title: Payment Information
payment.description: Payments are processed through Stripe. PROBATIO does not store credit card numbers, CVVs, or full card details on our servers. We retain only Stripe customer IDs, subscription status, and invoice records necessary for billing and account management.

usage.title: Usage Data
usage.description: We collect anonymized usage analytics through PostHog to improve the platform experience. This includes page views, feature usage patterns, and session duration. Analytics data is aggregated and cannot be used to identify individual users. You may opt out of analytics tracking through your account settings.

dataProcessing.title: 2. How We Process Your Data
dataProcessing.intro: When you upload audio for analysis, it passes through our deterministic ML pipeline. Understanding each step helps you know exactly what happens to your content:

pipeline.demucs: Source Separation — Neural separation engine isolates vocal, bass, drum, and other stems
pipeline.crepe: Pitch Extraction — Neural pitch tracker extracts frame-level fundamental frequency from vocal stems
pipeline.clap: Embedding Generation — Neural audio embedding model produces semantic representations for similarity search
pipeline.librosa: Feature Analysis — Spectral analysis engine extracts chroma, onset, beat, and structural features from each stem
pipeline.reports: Report Generation — Anthropic Claude API synthesizes human-readable analysis narratives from raw data
pipeline.musicbrainz: Rights Lookup — Music metadata enrichment service provides rights holder information for identified works

dataProcessing.gpuNote: All ML inference runs on Modal.com GPU infrastructure (NVIDIA A10G / A100). Processing is ephemeral — GPU containers are destroyed after each job. No audio data persists on GPU infrastructure.

thirdParty.title: 3. Third-Party Services
thirdParty.intro: PROBATIO relies on the following third-party services to operate. Each service receives only the minimum data necessary for its function:

services.supabase: Database, authentication, and encrypted file storage
services.modal: GPU compute for ML pipeline inference
services.anthropic: Natural language report generation via Claude API
services.vercel: Application hosting and global CDN delivery

dataRetention.title: 4. Data Retention
dataRetention.p1: Our data retention schedule is detailed in Section 5 of our Terms of Service. In summary: Screening audio is retained for 90 days, screening reports for 1 year, forensic data for the case duration plus 7 years, and reference library data is stored as embeddings only (no raw audio retained).
dataRetention.p2: When retention periods expire, data is permanently deleted using cryptographic erasure — encryption keys are destroyed, rendering stored ciphertext unrecoverable. Deletion is logged in our audit trail.

yourRights.title: 5. Your Rights
yourRights.intro: You have the following rights regarding your personal data and content stored on PROBATIO:

access.title: Right of Access.
access.description: You may request a complete copy of all personal data we hold about you, including account information, analysis history, and any associated metadata.

correction.title: Right of Correction.
correction.description: You may update or correct your account information at any time through your account settings, or by contacting us directly.

deletion.title: Right of Deletion.
deletion.description: You may request deletion of your account and all associated data. For Screening tier data, deletion is processed within 30 days. Forensic data subject to legal hold cannot be deleted until the hold is released — this exception exists to preserve evidence integrity.

export.title: Right of Data Export.
export.description: You may export your analysis reports, account data, and metadata in standard formats (JSON, CSV, PDF) at any time through your account settings.

optOut.title: Right to Opt Out of Analytics.
optOut.description: You may disable PostHog analytics tracking through your account privacy settings. This will not affect the functionality of the analysis platform.

security.title: 6. Security
security.intro: PROBATIO implements the following security measures to protect your data:

measures.tls: TLS 1.3 encryption for all data in transit between your browser, our servers, and third-party services
measures.encryption: AES-256 encryption at rest for all data stored in Supabase, including audio files, analysis results, and account information
measures.rls: Row-Level Security (RLS) database policies ensuring strict tenant isolation — users can only access their own data, enforced at the database layer
measures.audit: Append-only audit logs with cryptographic hash chaining (Merkle chain), protected by database triggers that prevent modification or deletion of any audit entry

children.title: 7. Children's Privacy
children.description: PROBATIO is not intended for use by individuals under the age of 18. We do not knowingly collect personal information from minors. If you believe a minor has created an account or submitted data to PROBATIO, please contact us at privacy@probatio.audio and we will promptly delete the associated data.

changes.title: 8. Changes to This Policy
changes.p1: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, technology, legal requirements, or business operations. When we make material changes — such as introducing new third-party processors, changing data retention periods, or modifying your rights — we will notify you via email at least 30 days before the changes take effect.
changes.p2: Non-material changes (such as clarifications or formatting improvements) may be made without advance notice but will be reflected in the "Last Updated" date at the top of this document.

contact.title: 9. Contact
contact.intro: For questions about this Privacy Policy, to exercise your data rights, or to report a privacy concern, contact us at:

privacy.closing: This Privacy Policy applies to all users of PROBATIO regardless of location. Where local privacy regulations provide additional rights beyond those described here, those additional rights apply. If any provision of this policy is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions continue in full force and effect.

standard.title: Compare Two Tracks
standard.description: Upload a track and compare against another. Standard similarity analysis with 4-dimension scoring.

clearance.title: Pre-Release Clearance
clearance.description: Scan your track against entire catalogs before release. Find potential conflicts automatically.

catalogSelector.heading: Select catalogs to scan against
catalogSelector.noCatalogsHint: Create a catalog and add reference tracks to enable clearance scanning.

report.heading: PRE-RELEASE CLEARANCE REPORT
report.noMatchesDescription: Your track did not match any reference tracks in the scanned catalogs.

cleared.label: CLEARED
cleared.description: No significant matches found. Safe to release.

conditional.label: CONDITIONAL
conditional.description: Moderate matches detected. Review before release.

blocked.label: BLOCKED
blocked.description: High-similarity matches found. Do not release without clearance.

statusDescription.cleared: No significant matches found. Safe to release.
statusDescription.conditional: Moderate matches detected. Review recommended before release.
statusDescription.blocked: High-similarity matches found. Do not release without clearance.

methodologyPage.title: Methodology
methodologyPage.subtitle: A comprehensive overview of Probatio's forensic analysis pipeline, designed for transparency, reproducibility, and Daubert compliance.

pipelineOverview.title: 1. Pipeline Overview
pipelineOverview.description: Probatio employs an eight-stage deterministic pipeline for audio similarity analysis. Each stage is independently auditable, with cryptographic hash verification at every boundary. The pipeline is versioned and pinned — the same input processed with the same pipeline version will always produce the same output.

normalization.title: 2. Normalization
normalization.description: All input audio is normalized to a canonical format before analysis to ensure comparability across different recording conditions. The normalization parameters are logged in the chain of custody.

sourceSeparation.title: 3. Source Separation
sourceSeparation.p1: Source separation isolates four stems — vocals, drums, bass, and other instruments — using a proprietary neural separation engine optimized for music production quality. This allows dimension-specific analysis: melodic comparison uses vocal stems, harmonic analysis uses the combined pitched content, and rhythmic analysis focuses on percussion patterns.
sourceSeparation.p2: The model version is pinned and logged. Deterministic mode is enforced via fixed random seeds and deterministic algorithms. Compute environment parameters are recorded for reproducibility verification.

featureExtraction.title: 4. Feature Extraction
featureExtraction.intro: Multiple feature extraction methods are applied to each stem:

pitch.label: Pitch contour:
pitch.description: Neural pitch tracking provides frame-level fundamental frequency estimation with confidence scores.

chroma.label: Chroma features:
chroma.description: Constant-Q chromagram captures harmonic content independent of octave.

onset.label: Onset detection:
onset.description: Spectral flux onset strength function identifies rhythmic event positions.

beat.label: Beat tracking:
beat.description: Dynamic programming beat tracker estimates tempo and beat positions.

structure.label: Structural segmentation:
structure.description: Spectral clustering identifies structural boundaries (verse, chorus, bridge).

similarityScoring.title: 5. Similarity Scoring
similarityScoring.intro: Similarity is computed across four dimensions, each using methods appropriate to the musical domain:

melody.title: Melody (weight: 0.35)
melody.description: Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) alignment of pitch contours extracted from vocal stems. Normalized by path length. Tolerant of key transposition up to 12 semitones.

harmony.title: Harmony (weight: 0.25)
harmony.description: Cross-correlation of chroma feature matrices. Captures chord progression similarity independent of instrumentation.

rhythm.title: Rhythm (weight: 0.20)
rhythm.description: Onset pattern correlation with tempo normalization. Identifies rhythmic figure similarity across different tempos.

timbre.title: Timbre (weight: 0.20)
timbre.description: Neural audio embedding comparison captures timbral and textural similarity at the production level.

riskClassification.title: 6. Risk Classification
riskClassification.intro: Overall similarity scores are classified into four risk levels using calibrated thresholds:
riskClassification.note: Threshold Version: 1.0.0. All thresholds are versioned and logged in the chain of custody for each analysis.

chainOfCustody.title: 7. Chain of Custody
chainOfCustody.intro: Every step in the analysis pipeline generates an audit log entry with:
entries: SHA-256 hash of the input data
SHA-256 hash of the output data
Link to previous audit entry (Merkle chain)
chainOfCustody.auditLogNote: The audit log is append-only — database triggers prevent modification or deletion of any entry. Each entry's hash incorporates the hash of the previous entry, creating a tamper-evident chain that can be independently verified via the public verification endpoint.

daubertCompliance.title: 8. Daubert Compliance
daubertCompliance.intro: Probatio's methodology is designed to satisfy the five Daubert factors for admissibility of expert testimony:

testability.label: 1. Testability and falsifiability:
testability.description: The deterministic pipeline can be independently verified. Same input + same version = same output.

peerReview.label: 2. Peer review:
peerReview.description: All underlying methods are grounded in peer-reviewed research in music information retrieval and signal processing. Pipeline methodology is publicly documented.

errorRate.label: 3. Known error rate:
errorRate.description: Classification accuracy is measured against known test corpora and reported with confidence intervals.

standards.label: 4. Standards and controls:
standards.description: Normalization, versioning, and hash verification ensure consistent application of the methodology.

acceptance.label: 5. General acceptance:
acceptance.description: Component methods (DTW, chroma analysis, spectral feature extraction) are widely accepted in the music information retrieval research community.

cta.title: Methodology Documentation
cta.description: Request the complete methodology whitepaper for legal review and Daubert compliance evaluation.

methodologyPage.disclaimer: This document describes the Probatio analysis methodology for informational purposes. While the methodology is designed to meet court evidentiary standards, the admissibility of any specific analysis is determined by the presiding judge in each proceeding. Probatio analysis does not constitute legal advice.

segmentAlignment.title: Segment Alignment Evidence
segmentAlignment.noEvidence: No segment-level evidence found. Overall similarity score: {score}

syncPlayer.title: Synchronized Playback

signing.verified: VERIFIED — This document was generated by Probatio and has not been modified.
signing.invalid: INVALID — This document's signature does not match. The file may have been modified.
signing.notFound: NOT FOUND — This document was not found in any Probatio chain of custody.
signing.dragDrop: Drag & drop a PDF or ZIP to verify its signature
signing.orPaste: Or paste the signature and hash below:

reproduce.title: Reproducibility Verification
reproduce.confirm: This will re-run the complete analysis pipeline to verify reproducibility. This typically takes 2-3 minutes. Proceed?
reproduce.match: REPRODUCIBLE — All steps produce matching outputs
reproduce.mismatch: MISMATCH — Some steps produced different outputs
reproduce.tolerance: Embeddings and scores from GPU inference may have minor floating-point variations (< 0.00001). This is expected and does not affect conclusions.
reproduce.planRequired: Reproduction requires a Professional or Enterprise plan.
reproduce.audioUnavailable: Original audio file no longer available. Cannot reproduce.
